faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in Spuyten Duyvil?
faqs.answer: Spuyten Duyvil's waterfront location, mature trees, and high concentration of older wooden homes create perfect conditions for carpenter ants. These pests are attracted to moisture-damaged wood, which is common in the neighborhood's historic properties and homes near the Hudson and Harlem Rivers. The area's lush vegetation also provides natural pathways for ants to access structures.

Larger homes or multi-unit buildings may require more extensive treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than typical household ants (up to 5/8 inch long) and are usually black or dark brown. Key signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) near wooden structures, faint rustling sounds inside walls, and seeing large winged ants indoors, especially in spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests, leaving clean, smooth galleries.
